attempts.strategy: Build the pure-tensor-algebra core `(A ⊗[R] R') ⊗[A] M ≃ₗ[R'] R' ⊗[R] M` on CANONICAL instances as `comm.toAddEquiv.trans (cancelBaseChange.toAddEquiv.trans comm.toAddEquiv)`, re-bundled as `≃ₗ[R']` by proving `map_smul'` on generators.
attempts.code_tried: comm ≪≫ cancelBaseChange ≪≫ comm; map_smul' via TensorProduct.induction_on with hsmul: r' • ((a⊗s)⊗m) = (a⊗(r'*s))⊗m from TensorProduct.smul_tmul' + algebraMap R' (A⊗[R]R') r' = 1⊗ₜr' (rfl) + tmul_mul_tmul
attempts.insight: cancelBaseChange is only B-linear in the M-factor; the needed R'-linearity (in the base-change factor) is NOT its declared linearity, so it must be re-proved by hand. The rightAlgebra instance gives r' • (a⊗s) = a⊗(r'*s). lean_verify: axioms [propext, Quot.sound] (no sorryAx).

next_steps: Promote to a blueprint helper block (lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv) — currently unmatched lean_aux coverage debt. It is the mathematical heart that, in a separately-compiled module, closes base_change_mate_regroupEquiv's hms sorry in one line.

findings.blocker: Carrier-instance wall: after TensorProduct.induction_on strips the restrictScalars object wrapper, the object R'-smul on the bare tensor carrier is opaque to every standard smul lemma (smul_zero/smul_add/restrictScalars.smul_def/ExtendScalars.smul_tmul all fail to match syntactically). The smul IS defeq to the canonical rightAlgebra action (rfl closes it) — a tactic/instance-matching wall, not a math gap.

attempts.strategy: Object-form ModuleCat R' iso in the restrictScalars/extendScalars packaging, built from g = comm ≫ (congr eT) ≫ cancelBaseChange ≫ comm with eT the identity A-linear bridge resolving the Module A (A ⊗[R] R') diamond; package via LinearEquiv.toModuleIso.
attempts.code_tried: letI instLHS/instRHS : Module R' (bare restrictScalars/extendScalars carriers); g additive composite; toModuleIso {g with map_smul' := hms}
attempts.lean_error: map_smul' (hms) unprovable in-file: induction_on strips the wrapper; `simp made no progress`, `rewrite did not find pattern ?a • (?b₁+?b₂)` across ~15 lean_run_code attempts
attempts.insight: Verified the object R'-smul is defeq to rightAlgebra smul. Clean closure exists: `exact LinearEquiv.toModuleIso (base_change_regroup_linearEquiv ↑M)` typechecks sorry-free ONLY when base_change_regroup_linearEquiv lives in a separately-compiled module (the imported type normalizes the diamond; same-file it does not reduce). Recommend splitting the helper into a new file.

next_steps: Plan agent: split base_change_regroup_linearEquiv (+ rightAlgebra/comm/cancelBaseChange plumbing) into a new imported file (e.g. Cohomology/RegroupHelper.lean) — closes hms outright. Auditor flagged the 'separate module' claim as unverifiable from source; the prover verified it end-to-end in a scratch import, so this is the recommended path but should be confirmed on first attempt.

attempts.insight: LocalizedModule.map S is Localization S-linear; map_exact is stated via IsLocalizedModule.map S (mkLinearMap …) and is defeq to LocalizedModule.map S, so it applies directly. Axiom-clean (propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound).

attempts.strategy: N_f is the localisation of N_{f'} at the image of f''; free transports across it via base-change cancellation. Build A_{f'} →+* A_{f'f''} (after rw [mul_comm] to supply IsLocalization.Away (f''*f')), make N_f an A_{f'}-module via compHom, discharge two scalar towers, show f' acts invertibly (Commute.isUnit_mul_iff), define IsLocalizedModule.lift, upgrade to A_{f'}-linear via extendScalarsOfIsLocalization, apply IsBaseChange.of_comp then .free.
attempts.code_tried: awayToAwayLeft f' f''; Module.compHom; IsLocalizedModule.lift; LinearMap.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization; (hbcA'.of_comp hbcAf).free
attempts.insight: The most complex L3 leaf (553-effort). Dead end avoided: the converse of IsLocalization.Away.mul' (IsLocalization.Away (algebraMap A A_{f'} f'') A_f) is NOT in Mathlib; IsBaseChange.of_comp sidesteps needing it. Axiom-clean.
